steps: At posting (day 0), there are 53 subscribers. Each day the count doubles, so after $d$ days the count is multiplied by 2 exactly $d$ times.
This is an exponential growth model with initial value 53 and growth factor 2.
The equation $n = 53(2)^d$ matches this pattern exactly.

steps.title: Graph the doubling checkpoints
steps.content: In the Desmos rows, $x_1$ lists days $0$ through $3$ and $y_1$ lists the subscriber counts after repeated doubling. The curve $y=53(2)^x$ should pass through each checkpoint.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '$n=(53)^d$': This raises 53 to the power $d$, which would mean at day 1 there are 53 subscribers, not double. Wrong structure — the base should be the growth factor, not the initial value. ✗
  • Choice B — '$n=53(2)^d$': Initial value 53, doubled each day (base 2). At $d=1$: $53 \cdot 2 = 106$ (double of 53). At $d=2$: $53 \cdot 4 = 212$ (double of 106). Correct. ✓
  • Choice C — '$n=53\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^d$': Base $\frac{1}{2}$ represents exponential decay (halving each day), not doubling. ✗
  • Choice D — '$n=(53)^2+d$': This is a linear function in $d$ added to a constant, not an exponential. It does not model daily doubling. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
